title: anthemis arvensis var. agrestis
intro: Anthemis arvensis var. agrestis, also known as corn chamomile or field chamomile, is a perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety, and promote relaxation. The health benefits of corn chamomile include its ability to calm the nervous system, reduce menstrual cramps, and ease insomnia, making it a popular herbal remedy for promoting overall well-being. Corn chamomile can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
